---
title: "1. Data Quality Assessment"
---

## Assessment approach

Before running any analysis, we systematically assessed the quality of each dataset along several dimensions. The table below summarizes the checks applied to each file.

| Check | Description |
|---|---|
| **File overview** | Row and column counts; identification of structurally empty columns |
| **Missing values** | Missing rate per variable, grouped by thematic block; flagging variables with meaningful missingness |
| **Duplicates** | Check for duplicate student or record identifiers |
| **Survey response quality** | Completion rate (`progress`) and response duration (`duration_min`) outliers |
| **Range validity** | Expected value ranges for all variables (Likert scales, binary indicators, continuous measures) |
| **Scale consistency** | Replication of pre-computed scale averages from constituent items; reversal checks for reverse-coded items |
| **Derived variable consistency** | Cross-variable logical checks (e.g., `educ_parent_max` vs. `educ_mother`/`educ_father`; math score totals vs. item sums) |

Additional dataset-specific checks are described within each section below. Thresholds were set on substantive grounds based on the study codebook and instrument documentation.

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")
import utils
import checks

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

utils.set_theme()

dfs = utils.load_all()
baseline  = dfs["baseline"]
schools   = dfs["schools"]
math_chat = dfs["math_chat"]
math_quiz = dfs["math_quiz"]
voc_chat  = dfs["voc_chat"]
voc_steps = dfs["voc_steps"]
```

---

## Data sources

The study draws on seven datasets from two sources: a Qualtrics baseline survey and interaction logs from the uDocz educational platform.

| Dataset | Grain | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline_estudiantes` | Student | Pre-treatment survey covering demographics, psychological scales, math assessment, and post-secondary aspirations |
| `randomizacion_escuelas` | School | School-level treatment assignment and stratification variables used for randomization |
| `math_chat_casa` | Message | Student–AI math tutoring conversations conducted at home |
| `math_chat_escuela` | Message | Student–AI math tutoring conversations conducted at school, with topic and correctness data |
| `math_ponte_prueba` | Quiz attempt | Math quiz interactions from the platform, with correctness indicators |
| `voc_chat` | Message | Student–AI career guidance conversations across an 8-step vocational journey |
| `voc_steps` | Student × step | Student-level progress, time spent, and completion across the 8 vocational journey steps |

---

## Student Baseline Data: Survey and Test

### A. File overview

Row and column counts; identification of structurally empty columns (conditional free-text fields never filled in).

```{python}
print(f"Rows: {len(baseline):,}  |  Columns: {len(baseline.columns)}")

empty_cols = checks.check_column_empty(baseline)
print(f"\nStructurally empty columns ({len(empty_cols)}):")
print(empty_cols[["variable"]].to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.callout-note}
The public dataset contains 1,203 students and 168 variables (183 in the raw data; 15 dropped during anonymization to remove personally identifiable information). Two variables are structurally empty (100% missing): `career_first_choice` and `action_3months`. Both are conditional free-text fields that were never filled in, likely because survey skip logic routed most respondents away from them. They are excluded from all subsequent checks.
:::

```{python}
baseline = baseline.drop(columns=empty_cols["variable"].tolist())
```

### B. Missing values

Missing rate per variable, grouped by thematic block. Structurally empty columns excluded.

```{python}
missing_results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_missing(baseline, cols=[c for c in cols if c in baseline.columns],
                         group_label=block)
    for block, cols in utils.BASELINE_VARS.items()
], ignore_index=True)

total_vars = sum(len(cols) for cols in utils.BASELINE_VARS.values())
vars_with_missing = len(missing_results)
print(f"{vars_with_missing} of {total_vars} variables have at least one missing value.")

utils.gt_missing(missing_results, title="Variables with missing values — Baseline survey")
```

::: {.callout-note}
Only 6 of 162 variables have missing values. Missing rates are low across the board: `educ_father` (8.5%) and `age`/`age_cat` (4.0%, same students) in the demographics block; and the three salary expectation variables (13.6%–16.0%) in the labor market beliefs block, reflecting students who skipped that module. All other variables are fully observed.
:::

### C. Duplicates

```{python}
dup_results = checks.check_duplicates(baseline, "qualtrics_id")
utils.gt_check_results(dup_results, title="Duplicate check — qualtrics_id")
```

::: {.callout-note}
No duplicate student identifiers found. Each of the 1,203 rows corresponds to a unique student.
:::

### D. Survey response quality

Flag incomplete surveys (`progress` < 100) and outliers in response duration.

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "progress", 100, 100),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "duration_min", 15, 60),
])
utils.gt_check_results(results, title="Survey response quality")
```

::: {.callout-warning}
Two response quality issues were identified. First, 23 students (1.9%) did not complete the survey — all stopped between 30–47% progress, suggesting dropout during a specific mid-survey section; their duration (mean: 36.7 min) is comparable to complete surveys (mean: 35.3 min), indicating these were not quick abandons. Second, 76 students (6.3%) fall outside the plausible duration range of 15–60 minutes: 54 completed too quickly (< 15 min, possibly inattentive) and 22 took too long (> 60 min, possibly distracted or interrupted). These students should be flagged as potentially low-quality responses in subsequent analyses.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Incomplete survey detail

```{python}
from scipy import stats

incomplete = baseline[baseline["progress"] < 100][["progress", "duration_min"]].sort_values("progress")
complete   = baseline[baseline["progress"] == 100]["duration_min"]

# Progress at dropout
col1 = incomplete["progress"].value_counts().sort_index().reset_index()
col1.columns = ["Progress (%)", "N Students"]
print("Progress at dropout:")
print(col1.to_string(index=False))

# Duration comparison + t-test
t_stat, p_val = stats.ttest_ind(incomplete["duration_min"], complete)
dur_comparison = pd.DataFrame({
    "Group": ["Incomplete (n=23)", "Complete (n=1,180)"],
    "Mean duration (min)": [
        round(incomplete["duration_min"].mean(), 1),
        round(complete.mean(), 1),
    ],
    "SD": [
        round(incomplete["duration_min"].std(), 1),
        round(complete.std(), 1),
    ],
})
print("\nDuration comparison:")
print(dur_comparison.to_string(index=False))
print(f"\nt-statistic: {t_stat:.3f}  |  p-value: {p_val:.3f}")
```
:::

```{python}
# Response quality flags for use in downstream analyses
baseline = baseline.copy()
baseline["flag_incomplete"] = (baseline["progress"] < 100).astype(int)
baseline["flag_duration"]   = ((baseline["duration_min"] < 15) | (baseline["duration_min"] > 60)).astype(int)
baseline["flag_low_quality"] = ((baseline["flag_incomplete"] == 1) | (baseline["flag_duration"] == 1)).astype(int)

print(f"flag_incomplete:  {baseline['flag_incomplete'].sum()} students")
print(f"flag_duration:    {baseline['flag_duration'].sum()} students")
print(f"flag_low_quality: {baseline['flag_low_quality'].sum()} students (union of both)")
```

### E. Demographics

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "age", 14, 19),
    checks.check_consistency(baseline, "female", "male",
                              label="female + male != 1"),
    checks.check_allowed_values(baseline, "educ_mother",
                                 allowed=list(range(1, 11)) + [12]),
    checks.check_allowed_values(baseline, "educ_father",
                                 allowed=list(range(1, 11)) + [12]),
    checks.check_binary(baseline, "has_computer"),
    checks.check_binary(baseline, "has_internet"),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "digital_comfort", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "platform_use", 1, 4),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### L. Academic background and perceptions

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_grade_prev", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "like_math", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "like_communication", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "mathconf_learn", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "mathconf_exam", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "mathconf_help_peers", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "mathconf_growth", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "rank_math", 1, 10),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "rank_communication", 1, 10),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### M. Computer lab

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "lab_productive", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "lab_preferred_hours", 1, 15),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### G. Hours budget and study habits

```{python}
HOURS_COLS = ["hours_school", "hours_study", "hours_friends", "hours_entertainment",
              "hours_housework", "hours_unpaid_work", "hours_paid_work",
              "hours_sleep", "hours_sport"]

hours_results = checks.check_hours_budget(
    baseline,
    hours_cols=HOURS_COLS,
    min_total=10,
    max_total=30,
    zero_exempt=["hours_unpaid_work", "hours_paid_work"],
    high_threshold=12,
)

print("--- Budget ---")
print(hours_results["budget"].to_string(index=False))
print("\n--- Unexpected zeroes ---")
print(hours_results["zeroes"].to_string(index=False))
print("\n--- Abnormally high values ---")
print(hours_results["high_values"].to_string(index=False))

# Study habit variables
study_results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_study_weekly", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "study_context", 1, 4),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "digital_tools_used", 1, 5),
])
print("\n--- Study habits ---")
print(study_results.to_string(index=False))
```

### F. Scale validity

Range checks on all items and replication of pre-computed averages. Reversal checks applied where items are reverse-coded.

```{python}
# ── Grit (1–5; grit_quit_game and grit_lose_interest reversed) ───────────────
grit_items = ["grit_challenge", "grit_quit_game", "grit_lose_interest", "grit_hardwork"]
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 5) for c in grit_items],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "grit_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_reversal(baseline,
                          direct_items=["grit_challenge", "grit_hardwork"],
                          reversed_items=["grit_quit_game", "grit_lose_interest"],
                          avg_col="grit_avg", scale_max=5),
])
print("Grit\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Growth mindset (1–6) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mindset_items = ["mindset_fixed1", "mindset_fixed2"]
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 6) for c in mindset_items],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "mindset_avg", 1, 6),
    checks.check_avg_replication(baseline, mindset_items, "mindset_avg"),
])
print("Growth mindset\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Locus of control (1–5) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
locus_items = ["locus_luck", "locus_fate", "locus_prepared", "locus_plans"]
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 5) for c in locus_items],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "locus_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_avg_replication(baseline, locus_items, "locus_avg"),
])
print("Locus of control\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Self-efficacy (1–4) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
efficacy_items = ["efficacy_hard_problems", "efficacy_effort", "efficacy_calm",
                   "efficacy_solutions", "efficacy_trouble"]
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 4) for c in efficacy_items],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "efficacy_avg", 1, 4),
    checks.check_avg_replication(baseline, efficacy_items, "efficacy_avg"),
])
print("Self-efficacy\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Math enjoyment (1–5; math_wish_no reversed) ───────────────────────────────
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 5)
      for c in ["math_enjoy", "math_wish_no", "math_like_problems"]],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_enjoy_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_reversal(baseline,
                          direct_items=["math_enjoy", "math_like_problems"],
                          reversed_items=["math_wish_no"],
                          avg_col="math_enjoy_avg", scale_max=5),
])
print("Math enjoyment\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Math anxiety (1–5) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_worry_grades", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_nervous_exams", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_anxiety_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_avg_replication(baseline,
                                  ["math_worry_grades", "math_nervous_exams"],
                                  "math_anxiety_avg"),
])
print("Math anxiety\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Math self-concept (1–5; math_harder_than_peers reversed) ─────────────────
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_does_well", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_harder_than_peers", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "math_selfconcept_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_reversal(baseline,
                          direct_items=["math_does_well"],
                          reversed_items=["math_harder_than_peers"],
                          avg_col="math_selfconcept_avg", scale_max=5),
])
print("Math self-concept\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Math attitudes standalone (math_useful: 1–4) ─────────────────────────────
print("Math attitudes\n",
      checks.check_range(baseline, "math_useful", 1, 4).to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Belonging (1–5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "belong_outsider", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "belong_fits_in", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "belonging_avg", 1, 5),
    checks.check_avg_replication(baseline,
                                  ["belong_outsider", "belong_fits_in"],
                                  "belonging_avg"),
])
print("Belonging\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Metacognition ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
meta_1_4 = ["meta_study_allocation", "meta_when_stuck", "meta_hint_use",
             "meta_review_errors", "meta_after_mistake", "meta_ai_use"]
results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 4) for c in meta_1_4],
    checks.check_range(baseline, "meta_expected_exam", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "meta_actual_exam", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "meta_self_awareness", 1, 3),
])
print("Metacognition\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Barriers (1–5) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
barrier_cols = ["barrier_money", "barrier_work_family", "barrier_parent_ideas",
                "barrier_grades", "barrier_info_careers", "barrier_info_institution",
                "barrier_family_resp"]
results = pd.concat([checks.check_range(baseline, c, 1, 5) for c in barrier_cols])
print("Barriers\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
# ── Knowledge of opportunities (1–3) ─────────────────────────────────────────
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "knows_scholarship", 1, 3),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "knows_beca18", 1, 3),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "knows_alt_training", 1, 3),
])
print("Knowledge of opportunities\n", results.to_string(index=False))
```

### N. Aspirations

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "barrier_finish_school", 1, 2),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "info_postsecondary", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "plan_after_school", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "plan_feasibility", 1, 5),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "knows_career", 1, 3),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "parent_educ_expectation", 1, 4),
    checks.check_binary(baseline, "enrolled_academy"),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "belief_univ_transition", 0, 10),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### O. Social network

```{python}
friend_cols = [f"friend{i}_{var}"
               for i in range(1, 6)
               for var in ["recess", "homework", "future_talk", "whatsapp"]]

results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "num_close_friends", 1, 6),
    *[checks.check_binary(baseline, c) for c in friend_cols if c in baseline.columns],
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### I. Salary beliefs

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_range(baseline, "salary_secondary", 1, 50000),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "salary_technical", 1, 50000),
    checks.check_range(baseline, "salary_university", 1, 50000),
    checks.check_ordering(baseline,
                          cols=["salary_secondary", "salary_technical", "salary_university"],
                          label="salary ordering violated (secondary <= technical <= university)"),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### H. Math assessment consistency

```{python}
correct_cols = ["correct_a1", "correct_a2", "correct_a7", "correct_a8", "correct_a8m",
                "correct_a4", "correct_a9s", "correct_a3", "correct_a6", "correct_a5"]

results = pd.concat([
    *[checks.check_binary(baseline, c) for c in correct_cols],
    checks.check_derived(
        baseline, "score_total_baseline",
        formula_fn=lambda df: df[correct_cols].sum(axis=1),
        label="score_total != sum of correct_a* items",
    ),
    checks.check_derived(
        baseline, "score_pct_baseline",
        formula_fn=lambda df: df["score_total_baseline"] / 10,
        label="score_pct != score_total / 10",
    ),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```

### J. Derived variable consistency

```{python}
results = pd.concat([
    checks.check_derived(
        baseline, "educ_parent_max",
        formula_fn=lambda df: df[["educ_mother", "educ_father"]].max(axis=1),
        label="educ_parent_max != max(educ_mother, educ_father)",
    ),
])
print(results.to_string(index=False))
```
